messages:
Welcome, {MC}.
I'm Ao — I host these sessions. Thank you for accepting the invitation. I know the description was vague; that's intentional, and I appreciate your trust in joining anyway.
A brief introduction, since you're the third to arrive today: Kō joined us this afternoon, and Haruki not long after. We're still expecting a few others over the coming days. Please make yourself comfortable.

hi {MC} 👋
ao that was so formal. is that the same speech you gave me
Word for word, Kō.
incredible. consistency. i respect it {MC} hi again, properly. i'm Kō. don't let the welcome packet scare you, it's nicer in here than it sounds
hi {MC} 🙇‍♂️ i'm haruki. i got here a couple hours ago so i am also extremely new, do not look to me for guidance

options.text: Hi everyone. Thanks for having me.
Three of us and Kō's already testing the host. Promising.
What exactly did I just sign up for?

messages: ao what happens when the last one goes out
That depends on the stories, Kō.
{MC} are you seeing this. two hours in and i still can't get a straight answer out of her
honestly i'm kind of glad she won't say. i feel like if she told us i'd just leave 😅

options.text: What kind of stories count? Urban legends? Family stuff?
Be honest — how scary are we talking?
Haruki, you're allowed to be a little scared. I think that's the point.

messages: {@MC} ok yeah you might be right {MC} for what it's worth — i joined on a dare from coworkers like four hours ago and i was 100% planning to leave after one night. but ao's actually really nice and kō is. well. kō is here. and i'm curious now

Any kind of story is welcome, {MC}. Folktales. Things passed down in your family. Something a friend swore happened to them. The only requirement is that you tell it sincerely. Stories told for effect tend not to land.
"tend not to land" is ao-speak for "the candle stays lit and it gets weird"
Kō.
sorry sorry 🦊 {MC} ignore me. ao runs a tight ship. you're in good hands
ok important onboarding question {MC}, since ao won't ask it horror tolerance. 1 to 10. 1 is "i sleep with the light on after a pixar movie", 10 is "please ruin my week" where are you

options.text: Maybe a 4. I scare easy, but I want to be here.

messages: {@MC} oh i like this one already ao take note. we have a good one

Noted, Kō. Though I'd ask you not to set expectations on {MC}'s behalf. {MC} — there is no obligation to tell a story tonight, or this week. Some participants prefer to listen for several sessions before contributing. That is entirely acceptable. The first lantern won't go out until someone is ready.
^ that is genuinely such a relief to hear bc i was sweating about this
haruki you've been sweating since you got here
correct and i will continue to do so 🫡

A few practical notes, {@MC}. The sessions take place in the evenings. Outside of those hours, this hall stays open for casual conversation — I encourage it. The people here are part of why the game works, and you'll get to know them better between sessions than during them. I'm counting on good stories from all of you. But more than that, I'm counting on honest ones. That's the part that matters.
ok that was kind of beautiful ao
she does that. saves it up all day and then drops one line and you have to sit with it
I don't "save it up," Kō.
sure 💙

options.text: This already feels like a good place to be.
Got it. Evenings for stories, the rest for getting to know everyone.
Ao — thank you. For the welcome.

messages: {@MC} I'm glad you're here, {MC}. The lanterns are lit. Tonight, you may simply listen.
oh {MC} heads up. that one is interesting
kō don't make it weird for {MC}'s first day
i'm not making it weird i'm making it informative
